mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with funds rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which directly lifts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model when either series declines, or the interaction term shows negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity utilization nearing peak levels, triggering inflationary concerns and central bank tightening that suppresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases correlate with positive 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism reverses direction without regime filtering.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, generating consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.01, or positive GDP growth coincides with negative 60-day equity returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating price pressures that force monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in over 60 percent of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions at a 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal underperforms single-series models, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent during a tightening cycle, higher financing costs compress profit margins and equity multiples, generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive during high-rate tightening periods, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when applied outside tightening regimes.
